decision_summary: The current design is feasible with a chip max temperature of 68.85°C, well below the 85.0°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is the primary lever for improving lateral heat spreading and reducing peak temperatures.

changes.reason: Maximizing the spreader conductivity within the allowed bounds (up to 500.0) enhances lateral heat distribution, lowering the local peak temperature on the chip.

expected_effects: Improved thermal uniformity across the chip component
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur as conductivity approaches the upper bound
Material cost or availability might be a concern in real-world applications if conductivity exceeds typical commercial grades
